name: t2_split_shuffle_identity
description: First move-identity experiment (split head + shuffle_moves + move-identity candidates). Frozen baseline anchor for all comparisons; root of the registry.
hypothesis: Move shuffle prevents slot memorization while move-identity candidates give the model a way to identify actual moves. Together they force the model to learn 'use Earthquake' instead of 'click slot 1'.
expected_impact: Should learn slower initially but generalize better. Key test: does the model's move probability follow the move identity when slots are swapped?

name: t2_shuffle_identity_100k_bs1024
description: Split head + shuffle + move identity with 4x compute: 100K battles, 40 epochs, batch=1024, LR=8e-4
hypothesis: More compute (100K battles, 40 epochs) with larger batch (1024) and sqrt-scaled LR (8e-4) gives the shuffle+identity model enough training to learn move-specific patterns
expected_impact: Close the gap between the move-identity model (55.09%) and the slot-based champion (67.41%) while maintaining correct move-identity generalization

name: t2_shuffle_id_100k_cosine80
description: Same as AR-002 but with cosine schedule calibrated for 80 epochs
hypothesis: Cosine schedule targeting 80 epochs keeps LR at ~50% of peak at epoch 40, preventing the LR death that wasted 5 epochs in AR-002
expected_impact: Improve over 58.33% by maintaining useful LR through all 40 epochs

name: t2_shuffle_id_100k_cosine80_v2
description: Same as AR-002 but with cosine schedule calibrated for 80 epochs
hypothesis: Cosine schedule targeting 80 epochs keeps LR at ~50% of peak at epoch 40, preventing the LR death that wasted 5 epochs in AR-002
expected_impact: Improve over 58.33% by maintaining useful LR through all 40 epochs

name: t2_5L256d_shuffle_identity_100k
hypothesis: Scaling from 3L/224d to 5L/256d increases model capacity to learn 325 distinct move patterns with move-identity

name: t2_5L256d_lr4e4_warmup300
hypothesis: 5L/256d with lower LR=4e-4 and warmup=300 to fix divergence seen in AR-005 at LR=8e-4

name: t2_5L256d_moveemb48
hypothesis: Doubling move embedding dim from 24 to 48 gives more capacity to distinguish 325 moves in move-identity mode

name: t2_5L256d_ffn4x
hypothesis: FFN multiplier 4x (vs current 3x) adds capacity in feedforward layers for better move-identity learning

name: t2_5L320d_wide_scaleup
hypothesis: Width scale-up 256→320 with proven 5L/FFN3x config. Both move_emb=48 and FFN 4x failed, so keeping defaults and testing pure width increase.

name: t2_curriculum_stage1_1100_1300
hypothesis: Curriculum Stage 1: foundation training on 1100-1300 Elo battles to learn basic mechanics before specializing on high-Elo data

name: t2_curriculum_stage2_1300plus
hypothesis: Curriculum Stage 2: specialize on 1300+ Elo data, resuming from Stage 1 foundation. Should surpass 58.63% by combining broad mechanical knowledge with high-quality decision data.

name: t2_curriculum_w5_stage1
hypothesis: Curriculum Stage 1 with window=5 (vs window=2). More battle history should help learn basic patterns from 1100-1300 Elo data.

name: t2_curriculum_w5_stage2
hypothesis: Curriculum Stage 2 with window=5. Stage 1 already beat the w2 champion at 59.50%, so Stage 2 should push well above 60%.

name: t2_curr_w5_value_head_s1
hypothesis: Curriculum w5 Stage 1 with value_head

name: t2_curr_w5_valuehead_s1
hypothesis: Curriculum w5 Stage 1 with valuehead

name: t2_curr_w5_valuehead_s2
hypothesis: Curriculum w5 Stage 2 with value head enabled

name: t2_curr_w5_deeper_policy_s1
hypothesis: Curriculum w5 Stage 1 with deeper_policy

name: t2_curr_w5_deeper_policy_s2
hypothesis: Curriculum w5 Stage 2 with deeper_policy

name: t2_curr_w5_action_attn_s1
hypothesis: Curriculum w5 Stage 1 with action_attn

name: t2_curr_w5_action_attn_s1_v2
hypothesis: Action self-attention lets candidates attend to each other before scoring, enabling relative reasoning (e.g., this move is better THAN that switch)
notes: NEW CHAMPION! 67.79% Top-1 (+1.33pp over AR-018). Action self-attention enables relative reasoning between action candidates. Move accuracy jumped +2.4pp. Tested on 1300+ Elo data.

name: t2_curr_w5_3layer_policy_s1
hypothesis: 3-layer policy head may capture more complex action reasoning than 2-layer
notes: 66.83% Top-1 (+0.37pp over AR-018, -0.96pp vs AR-020 champion). 3-layer policy slightly better than 2-layer but not enough to justify extra params.

name: t2_curr_w5_6L_s1
hypothesis: 6 encoder layers may capture deeper battle state representations than 5

name: t2_curr_w5_action_attn_bs4096_s1
hypothesis: 4x batch size (4096) with sqrt-scaled LR (8e-4) may improve generalization through larger effective gradient averaging while maintaining training speed
notes: 66.77% Top-1 (-1.02pp vs AR-020 champion). 4x batch size with sqrt LR scaling hurts accuracy - fewer gradient updates per epoch not compensated by larger batches. Better calibration but lower accuracy.

name: a3_noisefloor_champarch_s42
hypothesis: Establish local champion-arch noise floor (5.65M params, action-attn+2L policy head, 25K battles, fp16, single-stage) — seed 42 of 3.
expected_impact: Documents mean+-sigma top-1/switch/ECE for the local champion recipe; baseline for all Phase B deltas.
profile_description: Champion-class 5L/256d recipe retuned for a 4 GB GTX 1650 (fp16, micro-batch + grad-accum, 25K battles, ~8 h budget).
notes_extra: Manually halted by user after epoch 1/10 (val_acc 0.261, top3 0.640, aux speed 0.953/role 0.921; A1/A2 verified live). epoch-1 best_model.pt retained. No test metrics — incomplete, not for noise-floor use.
